Quick Start

Source: vignettes/quickstart.Rmd
quickstart.Rmd

This vignette gets you from installation to a first optimised PTSD diagnostic combination in about 5 minutes. For the full workflow — reliability analysis, ICD-11 comparison, model validation — see the Full Internal Analysis vignette.

Install & load 

# From CRAN
install.packages("PTSDdiag")

# Or the development version from GitHub
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("TobiasRSpiller/PTSDdiag")
library(PTSDdiag)

Load sample data

The package ships with simulated_ptsd: 5,000 simulated PCL-5 records (20 symptoms, 0–4 scale), structured to reflect a trauma-exposed clinical sample.

data("simulated_ptsd")
dim(simulated_ptsd)   # 5000 rows, 20 columns
#> [1] 5000   20
head(simulated_ptsd[, 1:5])
#>   S1 S2 S3 S4 S5
#> 1  2  1  4  1  4
#> 2  3  3  4  3  3
#> 3  0  1  1  2  2
#> 4  3  3  3  3  3
#> 5  2  3  3  2  3
#> 6  1  1  2  1  2

Prepare & score

Standardise column names to the symptom_1–symptom_20 convention, then calculate total scores and apply DSM-5-TR criteria:

df <- rename_ptsd_columns(simulated_ptsd)
df <- calculate_ptsd_total(df)
df <- create_ptsd_diagnosis_nonbinarized(df)

summarize_ptsd(df)
#>   mean_total sd_total n_diagnosed
#> 1     57.772 12.36218        4710

Find an optimal symptom combination

optimize_combinations() searches for the best k-symptom combinations (here: 4 out of 20) that preserve DSM-5-TR diagnostic accuracy. Setting score_by = "newly_nondiagnosed" minimises missed diagnoses (false negatives).

Note on computation time. This example uses n_symptoms = 4 (4,845 combinations) for speed. A typical research analysis uses n_symptoms = 6 (38,760 combinations), which takes 1–2 minutes.

result <- optimize_combinations(
  rename_ptsd_columns(simulated_ptsd),
  n_symptoms    = 4,
  n_required    = 3,
  n_top         = 3,
  score_by      = "newly_nondiagnosed",
  show_progress = FALSE
)

Top combinations found:

result$best_symptoms
#> [[1]]
#> [1]  1  6  7 13
#> 
#> [[2]]
#> [1]  4  6  7 13
#> 
#> [[3]]
#> [1]  4  6  7 17

Diagnostic accuracy metrics:

knitr::kable(result$summary, digits = 4,
             caption = "Top 3 non-hierarchical combinations")
Top 3 non-hierarchical combinations
Scenario combination_id rank Total Diagnosed Total Non-Diagnosed True Positive True Negative Newly Diagnosed Newly Non-Diagnosed True Cases False Cases Sensitivity Specificity PPV NPV
PTSD_orig NA NA 4710 (94.2%) 290 (5.8%) 4710 290 0 0 5000 0 1.0000 1.0000 1.0000 1.0000
symptom_1_6_7_13 1_6_7_13 1 4573 (91.46%) 427 (8.54%) 4535 252 38 175 4787 213 0.9628 0.8690 0.9917 0.5902
symptom_4_6_7_13 4_6_7_13 2 4574 (91.48%) 426 (8.52%) 4535 251 39 175 4786 214 0.9628 0.8655 0.9915 0.5892
symptom_4_6_7_17 4_6_7_17 3 4568 (91.36%) 432 (8.64%) 4528 250 40 182 4778 222 0.9614 0.8621 0.9912 0.5787
